help.overview: Close Objects Search sweeps the entire catalog for pairs of objects flying within 50 km of each other right now. It is a quick way to spot co-orbiting payloads, recent breakups, and crowded neighborhoods without running a full conjunction screening.
help.imgAlt: The Close Objects Search menu with its find button
help.imgCaption: One click sweeps the whole catalog for objects within 50 km of each other.
help.method: The search runs in two phases. First, a fast bounding box pass compares every object's current position to find candidate pairs within the search radius. Then each candidate pair is re-propagated 30 minutes into the future: pairs that are still close are confirmed, which filters out objects that merely crossed paths for a moment.
help.howToUse:
1. Open Close Objects from the bottom menu.
  2. Click Find Close Objects. The sweep takes a few seconds on the full catalog.
  3. The confirmed pairs are loaded into the search bar, so every close object is highlighted and listed in the search results.
  4. Click any result to inspect an object, or use other menus (like Debris Screening) to analyze a specific pair in depth.
help.tip1: Pairs are confirmed only if they remain within range 30 minutes later, so formation flyers and rendezvous pairs show up while brief crossings are filtered out.
help.tip2: The results replace the current search; copy the search text first if you need to keep an existing list.
